name: Santa Ana, Almonaster, and Castaño del Robledo Loop
name_original: Santa Ana la Real, Castaño del Robledo,  Almonaster la Real – ruta circular
description: A difficult 19.3-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Aracena, gaining 2955 feet with scenic views and white villages.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Santa Ana La Real traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and historic villages within the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park. The region offers varied terrain, from gentle paths through cork oak forests to more challenging ascents and descents. Elevations are moderate, providing a mix of scenic rides and technical sections. Best mountain bike trails around Santa Ana La Real The most popular mountain bike trail is Santa Ana, Almonaster, and Castaño del Robledo Loop, a 19.3 miles (31 km) difficult trail that takes 3 hours 17 minutes to complete, featuring significant elevation changes through rural landscapes. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Urraleda Trail – Castaño del Robledo loop from Jabugo, a moderate 13.5 miles (22 km) path. This route winds through forested areas and offers views of traditional villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Linares de la Sierra – Historic Center of Aracena loop from Sierra de Grijona, a 27.7 miles (45 km) trail leading through varied terrain including historic centers and natural parkland, often completed in about 4 hours 3 minutes. Mountain biking around Santa Ana La Real is defined by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and historic village paths. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult rides, catering to various skill levels. The routes in Santa Ana La Real are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 15 reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Santa Ana La Real's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Santa Ana La Real?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Santa Ana La Real, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. The region is known for its rolling hills and dense woodlands within the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy mountain bike trail available. The area's varied terrain includes gentle paths through cork oak forests, which can be suitable for less experienced riders or families looking for a relaxed outing.

Q: What are the typical lengths and durations of mountain bike trails in this area?
A: Trail lengths vary significantly. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38274467" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosque of Almonaster la Real loop from Almonaster la Real&lt;/a&gt; is about 7 miles (11 km) and takes around 1 hour 10 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/27628124" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Linares de la Sierra – Historic Center of Aracena loop from Sierra de Grijona&lt;/a&gt; can be up to 28 miles (45 km) and take over 4 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails around Santa Ana La Real are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e995550644/santa-ana-almonaster-and-castano-del-robledo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana, Almonaster, and Castaño del Robledo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10318399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urraleda Trail – Castaño del Robledo loop from Jabugo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the mountain bike trails?
A: The terrain around Santa Ana La Real is characterized by rolling hills, dense woodlands, and paths through historic villages. You'll encounter a mix of singletrack, gravel roads, and some paved sections, with moderate elevations providing both scenic rides and technical challenges.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Santa Ana La Real?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 15 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the beautiful natural parkland, and the opportunity to explore charming historic villages along the routes.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. You can find stunning views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2894628" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alájar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4184233" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arias Montano Peak and the Hermitage of Our Lady of the Angels&lt;/a&gt;, which are accessible from various trails.

Q: Can I find any waterfalls while mountain biking in Santa Ana La Real?
A: Yes, the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park features natural attractions, including a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7287170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails may pass near or offer detours to this natural monument, providing a refreshing stop during your ride.

Q: What are some interesting historical or cultural sites to see near the trails?
A: The region is rich in history. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4550593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mosque of Almonaster la Real&lt;/a&gt;, a unique historical landmark, or explore traditional settlements like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5301554" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castaño del Robledo&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4090741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Marines Village&lt;/a&gt;, which are often integrated into the mountain bike routes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Santa Ana La Real?
A: Spring and autumn are generally considered the best times for mountain biking in Santa Ana La Real. The weather is milder, and the natural landscapes are at their most vibrant. Summers can be hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters are typically mild but can be wet.

Q: Are there any challenging or difficult mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40637270" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urraleda Trail – Puerto de Alájar (820 m) loop from Fuenteheridos&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and technical sections. There are 15 difficult routes in the area, providing plenty of options for advanced mountain bikers.
tips: The Camino de Santa Ana to Alájar is a popular hiking route in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche, in the province of Huelva. This trail connects the towns of Santa Ana la Real and Alájar, offering a picturesque route through natural landscapes and points of historical interest.😊

The Castaño del Robledo farmhouses have been declared a Historic-Artistic Site. The oldest farmhouse is in the vicinity of the Plaza El Álamo, where the church of Santiago is also located, a very characteristic point of the town.
Its own hallmark in the Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park is the Cerro de San Cristóbal, in the municipality of Almonaster la Real.

In recent decades this medieval monument has been appreciated and with good reason! It is the old Muslim mosque, later converted into the Christian Hermitage of the Conception of Our Lady. There are records of its existence for more than 1,700 years, and it is supposed to have been built on top of some monumental Roman building from the 1st or 2nd century AD.

faqs.question: What is the estimated duration for completing this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular mountain bike route is approximately 31 km long with around 900 meters of elevation gain. Komoot estimates an average completion time of just over 3 hours, but as it's rated 'difficult', your actual duration will depend on your fitness level and technical riding skills.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly rated as 'difficult' and is not suitable for beginners. It requires very good fitness and advanced riding skills. Be prepared for significant ascents and descents, and some portions may even require you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular trail connecting Santa Ana la Real, Castaño del Robledo, and Almonaster la Real. You can typically find parking in or near any of these villages to start your ride. Specific trailhead parking details are not provided, but village centers usually offer public parking options.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and challenging, reflecting the geological diversity of the Sierra de Aracena. You'll encounter surfaces influenced by slate, sandstone, granite, and limestone. While specific trail types (singletrack, fire roads) aren't detailed, the 'difficult' rating suggests a mix of demanding and potentially technical sections, possibly including rough or uneven surfaces.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll ride through lush, green landscapes of holm oak, cork oak, olive, and extensive chestnut forests. The route offers panoramic views of the Sierra and passes through picturesque 'white villages'. Notable landmarks include the Plaza del Álamo and Church of Santiago in Castaño del Robledo, the Cerro de San Cristóbal, and the historic Mosque of Almonaster la Real. You'll also traverse sections of the Santa Ana to Alájar Trail.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to ride this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park is beautiful year-round, but for mountain biking, spring (March-May) and autumn (September-November) generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and conditions, avoiding the intense heat of summer and potential winter rains.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the presence of wildlife like genet, deer, and wild boar, and Europe's largest breeding colony of black vultures. The challenging nature of this specific route also means your dog needs to be fit enough to keep up with a difficult mountain bike ride.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for mountain biking on public trails within the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park. However, always respect local regulations, stay on marked trails, and practice Leave No Trace principles.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the 'difficult' rating, you should bring a well-maintained mountain bike suitable for technical terrain, a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water and snacks, a repair kit (including spare tubes/patch kit), a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone with the Komoot route downloaded for offline navigation.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route connects the villages of Santa Ana la Real, Castaño del Robledo, and Almonaster la Real. All three offer options for cafes, pubs, and accommodation, allowing you to refuel or stay overnight before or after your ride.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this circular route utilizes sections of several other trails within the park. You'll find yourself on parts of 'Las Cuatro Riveras', 'Santa Ana la Real-Castaño del Robledo', 'Risco Levante-Los Chorros', 'Tierra de los Descubrimientos', and 'Las Aldeas de Poniente', among others.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the Sierra de Aracena y Picos de Aroche Natural Park?
faqs.answer: The park is rich in biodiversity. While riding, you might spot various birds, including black vultures (it hosts Europe's largest breeding colony). Mammals such as genet, deer, and wild boar also inhabit the area, though they are often shy and harder to spot.

waypoints.name: Plaza del Álamo and Church of Santiago, Castaño del Robledo
